summary: Building an esports app named Legend X Esports, inspired by a Google Sites page shared by the user.
known_issues: Logo was not updated/changed as expected by the user.
imageRequirements:
## Edit Images
- Resize/crop to 512×512 for profile avatar use (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-4.jpg > profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png)
- Resize/crop to 192×192 for PWA icon use (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-4.jpg > pwa-icon.dim_192x192.png)
- Resize/crop to 512×512 for PWA icon use (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-4.jpg > pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png)

existing_features.title: Build a public-facing esports organization landing page for 'Legend X Esports' with the following sections: Hero (team name, tagline, call-to-action), About Us, Our Team / Roster (player cards with name and role), News / Announcements, and a Contact / Social Links footer.
Connect the frontend to the backend canister using React Query: fetch and display live roster and announcements data from the Motoko actor.

Apply a bold, dark esports visual theme across the entire application: dark background (near-black), vivid accent color (e.g. electric red or neon gold), aggressive typography with strong hierarchy, subtle glowing or metallic UI elements, and a consistent grid-based layout. The theme should feel high-energy and professional.

Use the uploaded Free Fire battle artwork image as the background image for the HeroSection component. Save it as a static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png and apply it as the full-screen background of the hero section, with a dark overlay to ensure text readability.

In the Footer component, update the YouTube social media icon link to point to https://youtube.com/@mrqlegendff?si=IUBFSTd9J-PGWeNQ so that clicking the YouTube icon opens the Legend X Esports YouTube channel.

In the HeroSection component, reduce the zoom / scale of the hero background image so it is less zoomed-in and more of the scene is visible. Adjust the background-size, background-position, or CSS transform/scale properties on the background image or its container to achieve a more zoomed-out appearance while keeping the overlay and text readable.

In the HeroSection component, adjust the hero background image (frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png) so it is displayed with a portrait / mobile-screen aspect ratio — fitted from the sides (left and right edges constrained) so the image fills the section vertically like a mobile screen, without horizontal overflow. Use CSS background-size, background-position, or an with object-fit/object-position so the image behaves as if it were cropped and fitted to a tall, narrow (portrait) viewport, centred horizontally. The dark overlay and all text content must remain fully readable on top.

Save the uploaded profile image as a static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png and use it as the organization's profile/brand avatar throughout the app: display it in the Navbar (replacing or alongside the text logo) as a small circular avatar, and display it prominently in the AboutSection as the team's brand profile image.

Save the newly uploaded gaming artwork image (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-1.jpg — a masked figure in a white turtleneck with a Gucci cap, neon red halo, red diamond YouTube play button, and dark esports background) as the new profile avatar static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png, replacing the existing file. This asset is already referenced in the Navbar (circular avatar) and AboutSection (brand profile image), so both components will automatically display the new image without any code changes.

Add a medium-sized glowing button in the Navbar component that links to https://sites.google.com/view/mrqlegendyt/home. The button should open the link in a new tab, use the site's accent color (red/gold glow), pulse or glow on hover, and be clearly visible in the top navigation bar on both desktop and mobile layouts. Label it something like 'Visit Website' or 'Our Site'.

Implement a PWA install prompt that appears prominently at the very top of the page (above the Navbar) as a full-width banner. The banner must display the text 'Download App' (or 'Install App') with a clear call-to-action button. When a user opens the link, this banner should be the first thing they see, giving them confidence to install before proceeding. The banner must use the site's accent colors (red/gold) and match the existing dark esports theme. On desktop and mobile, it should be clearly visible and dismissible.

In the frontend, locate the element identified by the XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5]/span[1] — which is a <span> inside the About section — and replace its text content with 'Mr Q Legend YT'.

Apply a consistent text shadow and subtle glow effect to all visible text elements across every frontend component (HeroSection, Navbar, AboutSection, RosterSection, PlayerCard, NewsSection, AnnouncementCard, Footer, PWAInstallBanner, and LandingPage). Define reusable CSS utility classes in frontend/src/index.css (e.g. a 'text-glow' class using text-shadow with the site's accent color — red or gold) and apply them globally via Tailwind's base layer or a global selector so that every heading, paragraph, label, button text, and span benefits from improved contrast and visibility against dark backgrounds.

Save the newly uploaded gaming artwork image (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-2.jpg — a masked figure in a white turtleneck with a Gucci cap, neon red halo, red diamond YouTube play button, and dark esports background) as the PWA app icon assets. Generate icon variants at the required PWA sizes (192×192 and 512×512) and save them as static assets at frontend/public/assets/generated/pwa-icon.dim_192x192.png and frontend/public/assets/generated/pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png respectively.

Update frontend/public/manifest.json to reference the new PWA icon files (pwa-icon.dim_192x192.png and pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png) for the 'icons' array entries at sizes 192×192 and 512×512, replacing any previously referenced icon paths. Also set these icons as the splash screen / maskable icons so they appear on the home screen and app launch screen when the PWA is installed.

Save the uploaded red/black hexagonal mesh artwork image (quality_restoration_20260224212439860.jpg) as the new hero background static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png, replacing the existing file. Update the HeroSection component to use this new image as its full-screen background, maintaining the existing dark overlay so all text and CTA elements remain fully readable on top.

Save the uploaded yellow-to-red polygonal low-poly geometric artwork image (quality_restoration_20260224213607352.jpg) as the new hero background static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png, replacing the existing file. Update the HeroSection component to use this new image as its full-screen background, maintaining the existing dark overlay so all text and CTA elements remain fully readable on top.

Save the newly uploaded gaming artwork image (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-3.jpg — a masked figure in a white turtleneck with a Gucci cap, neon red halo, red diamond YouTube play button, and dark esports background) as the new profile avatar static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png, replacing the existing file.

Audit all frontend components (Navbar, AboutSection, RosterSection, PlayerCard, Footer, HeroSection, NewsSection, AnnouncementCard, PWAInstallBanner, LandingPage) for any remaining references to the old 'neez3z' placeholder image or any hardcoded third-party avatar URLs, and replace them with /assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png so the user's own picture is used consistently everywhere.

In the Navbar component, replace the current text-based or placeholder logo with the profile avatar image at /assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png. Display it as a circular avatar (or small logo image) on the left side of the navigation bar, clearly visible on both desktop and mobile layouts. Remove or update any existing logo text/icon that was not reflecting the user's own image.

Save the newly uploaded gaming artwork image (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-4.jpg — a masked figure in a white turtleneck with a Gucci cap, neon red halo, red diamond YouTube play button, and dark esports background) as the new profile avatar static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png, replacing the existing file.

Generate a 192×192 px variant of the newly uploaded image (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-4.jpg) and save it as frontend/public/assets/generated/pwa-icon.dim_192x192.png, replacing the existing PWA icon at that size.

Generate a 512×512 px variant of the newly uploaded image (quality_restoration_20260128182709920-4.jpg) and save it as frontend/public/assets/generated/pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png, replacing the existing PWA icon at that size.

Audit all frontend components (Navbar, AboutSection, RosterSection, PlayerCard, Footer, NewsSection, AnnouncementCard, PWAInstallBanner, LandingPage) and ensure every location that displays a profile image, player avatar, or logo uses /assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png. The hero background image (hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png) must NOT be changed.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a Motoko backend actor that stores and serves: team announcements (title, body, timestamp) and roster entries (name, role, avatarUrl). Expose query functions to retrieve all announcements and all roster members, and update functions to add or modify entries.
Add a background image to the app (hero/main background picture).
Link the YouTube icon to the channel https://youtube.com/@mrqlegendff
Zoom out / reduce zoom level of the hero background image
Update the year '2024' to '2026' in the app (likely in the footer copyright or similar text).
In the Footer component, find all occurrences of the year '2024' (e.g. in the copyright notice or any other text) and replace them with '2026'.
Adjust the hero background image to fit like a mobile screen ratio — fit it sideways (cover/contain adjusted so it resembles a mobile screen aspect ratio, fitted from the sides).
Display the profile avatar image on the profile/player card(s) in the roster or team section.
Add a medium-sized glowing button linking to https://sites.google.com/view/mrqlegendyt/home that is visible at the top of the page (e.g. in the Navbar or Hero section).

Add a prominent 'Download App' button/prompt so that when users open the link, they are first shown a download option. The app should be installable as a standalone app (PWA with install prompt) without needing to visit the website separately — displayed clearly at the top with a 'Download App' label.
Update the app text to display 'Mr Q Legend YT' at a specific location the user pointed to.
Add text shadow and subtle glow effect to all text elements across the app to improve readability and visibility.
Use the profile avatar image (profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png) as the PWA app icon — so when the app is installed as a PWA, the home screen icon and splash screen show this image.
Use the PWA app icon image (pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png or profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png) as the hero/main background image of the app, replacing the current hero background.
Replace all instances of the 'neez3z' image throughout the app with the user's own profile picture (profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png).

In the Footer component, locate the element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/footer[1]/div[3]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/img[1] — which is the 'neez3z' / placeholder avatar image in the footer — and replace its src with the profile avatar at /assets/generated/profile-avatar.dim_512x512.png so the user's own picture is displayed there.
Change/update the logo in the app (user reports logo was not changed as expected).

Use the uploaded profile picture everywhere in the app where there are profile images or logos (except the hero background). This includes: all player/profile cards, the PWA app icon (home screen icon after install), and all navbar/logo locations. The hero background should remain unchanged.
